Heatwave: SDMA issues multi-hazard advisory for public
Vijayawada: AP State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) managing director Prakhar Jain urged citizens to adopt preventive measures against extreme heat, heatwaves, thunderstorms, and lightning to avoid life‑threatening situations.
Jain explained that symptoms such as headache, dizziness, high fever, confusion, and seizures may indicate heatstroke. He advised using caps, scarves, light‑coloured cotton clothing, and sunglasses for protection. To keep homes cool, people should apply white paint on rooftops, adopt cool roof technology, ensure cross‑ventilation, and use insulation. Growing terrace and indoor plants can also help reduce heat.
